signals.description: Company-wide restructuring with layoffs affecting 2-15% of workforce across 13 countries, pivoting from aggressive market expansion to operational efficiency
Q4 2025 revenue of $1.99B (+43% YoY), but 2026 guidance of $6.5-6.9B missed analyst expectations of $7.3B, stock fell sharply
Strategic pivot to $10B prediction/event-contract market; redirecting capital from sports betting expansion to new verticals

prospects.summary: DraftKings is in the ICP sweet spot at ~$6.7B revenue and 5,500 employees. The company is undergoing a major strategic pivot — shifting from aggressive market expansion to profitability and entering the prediction markets space. The combination of restructuring, earnings guidance miss, and strategic transformation creates a strong need for organizational alignment and cross-functional coordination, core McChrystal Group strengths.
prospects.entry_point: Jason Park, Chief Transformation Officer — created specifically to coordinate the restructuring, Super App launch, and acquisition integrations. Natural buyer for organizational alignment services. Linda Aiello (CPO) as secondary entry for leadership development.
prospects.conversation_hook: DraftKings is simultaneously restructuring across 13 countries, launching a Super App combining four product lines, and integrating two acquisitions — all against a March Madness deadline. The CTO role signals they know coordination is the bottleneck.
prospects.company_overview: DraftKings is a digital sports entertainment and gaming company operating one of the largest online sportsbooks and daily fantasy sports platforms in North America. The company went public via SPAC in 2020 and has rapidly expanded into mobile sports betting as states legalize.

signals.description: Major restructuring to reduce employee roles by 20% by end of 2027; consolidating activities to operating sites with 200 positions migrating to Houston
Leveraging parent ExxonMobil's technology and global capabilities; offshoring 360 positions to global business centers in India, Argentina, Thailand, Brazil

prospects.summary: Imperial Oil is a strong ICP fit in the energy sector with ~5,100 employees undergoing a 20% workforce reduction and major organizational consolidation. The restructuring involves cross-border role migration and integration with ExxonMobil's global operations — exactly the kind of complex, multi-site transformation where Team of Teams methodology excels. The $150M annual savings target and $330M restructuring charge signal serious organizational change.
prospects.entry_point: CEO or COO — driving the 20% workforce reduction and cross-border role migration to Houston. HR leadership as secondary entry given the scale of people impact across Canada, India, Argentina, Thailand, Brazil.
prospects.conversation_hook: Imperial Oil is eliminating 20% of roles by 2027 while offshoring 360 positions to four countries and consolidating to operating sites — a multi-geography transformation where siloed execution will destroy value.
prospects.company_overview: Imperial Oil is one of Canada's largest integrated oil companies, with operations spanning oil sands production, conventional crude oil, petroleum refining, and fuel marketing. Majority-owned by ExxonMobil, the company operates the Kearl oil sands mine and Strathcona refinery.

signals.description: Emerged from Chapter 11 on Sept 29, 2025 after 91-day prepackaged restructuring; eliminated $4.6B in debt, reduced workforce by 20%
Transitioning from legacy 150mm to 200mm silicon carbide production; closed Durham NC fab, consolidating to new Mohawk Valley mega-fab

prospects.summary: Wolfspeed just emerged from Chapter 11 as a leaner organization with 70% less debt and a new operational footprint. The company is in a critical post-restructuring phase — rebuilding culture, aligning a reduced workforce around a new technology roadmap (200mm SiC), and executing a facility consolidation. This is a window where leadership development and organizational transformation consulting can have outsized impact.
prospects.entry_point: CEO (post-Chapter 11 leadership) — rebuilding organizational identity with a leaner workforce after 20% cuts. CHRO as champion for culture rebuild post-bankruptcy.
prospects.conversation_hook: Wolfspeed emerged from Chapter 11 just months ago with 70% less debt and a shuttered Durham fab. The post-restructuring window — rebuilding culture, aligning a reduced workforce around 200mm SiC — is exactly when transformation consulting has outsized impact.
prospects.company_overview: Wolfspeed is a global leader in silicon carbide (SiC) semiconductor technology, producing power devices and materials critical to electric vehicles, renewable energy, and 5G infrastructure. Spun off from Cree, the company is investing $6.5B+ to build next-generation manufacturing capacity.

signals.description: Q3 2025 revenue $743M, down 14% YoY; Adjusted EBITDA fell 50%+ from $66M to $30M; FY2025 net loss forecast of $408-418M
Closing German polystyrene asset, repurposing Italian MMA facility; company-wide cost restructuring targeting $30M annualized savings
Pivoting to higher-value recycled PMMA products; $60-70M restructuring spend over three years to reposition business

prospects.summary: Trinseo is a mid-market specialty materials company in significant distress — revenue declining 14%, EBITDA halved, and projecting a $400M+ net loss. The company is simultaneously restructuring operations across Europe and pivoting its product strategy toward sustainability. This level of organizational stress combined with strategic transformation is where McChrystal Group's leadership and adaptability frameworks can help management navigate complexity.
prospects.entry_point: CEO — navigating simultaneous operational restructuring across Europe and strategic pivot to recycled PMMA. CFO as secondary given $60-70M restructuring spend requiring ROI justification.
prospects.conversation_hook: Trinseo is projecting a $400M+ net loss while closing German assets, repurposing Italian facilities, and pivoting product strategy — this level of organizational stress with a 3-year restructuring timeline needs a framework, not just cost cuts.
prospects.company_overview: Trinseo is a specialty materials company producing plastics, latex binders, and synthetic rubber for automotive, construction, consumer electronics, and packaging markets. Formerly part of Dow Chemical, the company operates manufacturing sites across the Americas, Europe, and Asia.

signals.description: Announced separation into two independent publicly traded companies — Global Automotive (NAPA) and Global Industrial (Motion) — by Q1 2027
Achieved $175M in 2025 cost savings from restructuring, targeting additional $100-125M in 2026; global supply chain optimization across 17 countries and 10,800+ locations

prospects.summary: GPC is above the ICP revenue sweet spot at $24.3B, but the planned separation into two independent companies creates a massive organizational transformation challenge. Splitting 58,000 employees across 17 countries into two autonomous entities requires exactly the kind of cross-functional coordination and leadership alignment McChrystal Group specializes in. The separation timeline (Q1 2027) means planning is happening now. ---
prospects.entry_point: CEO or Chief Strategy Officer — leading the planned separation into two independent public companies (NAPA and Motion). CHRO as champion given 58,000 employees across 17 countries need to be split.
prospects.conversation_hook: GPC is separating into two independent publicly traded companies by Q1 2027 — splitting 58,000 employees across 17 countries into autonomous entities is a once-in-a-decade organizational design challenge happening right now.
prospects.company_overview: Genuine Parts Company (GPC) is a global distributor of automotive and industrial replacement parts, operating NAPA Auto Parts in North America and Alliance Automotive Group in Europe. Founded in 1928, GPC serves over 600,000 customers through 10,000+ distribution points worldwide.

signals.description: Revenue surging 28% YoY — raised FY2026 guidance to $5.9-6.3B from $4.8B in FY2025; driven by AI and cloud infrastructure demand
Restructuring with reductions in force, facility optimization, and business process redesign; $20M restructuring charge in Q3 2025
Scaling from optical networking into AI infrastructure platform; Blue Planet software segment shifting to operating loss as company pivots business model

prospects.summary: Ciena is experiencing explosive 28% YoY growth driven by AI infrastructure demand, creating exactly the coordination challenges McChrystal Group addresses. At $5.9B revenue and 9,000 employees, the company is restructuring operations while simultaneously pivoting its business model toward AI — a classic 'scaling pain' scenario where Team of Teams methodology helps fast-growing organizations maintain alignment.
prospects.entry_point: Gary Smith, CEO (24-year tenure) — managing a fundamentally different company than the one he built. Sheela Kosaraju (SVP/GC serving as interim CPO) as secondary — she's dual-hatting during the most significant talent scaling in Ciena's history.
prospects.conversation_hook: Ciena just rejoined the S&P 500 with 28% revenue growth, but three customers are 67% of revenue, there's no permanent CPO, and R&D/supply chain/sales are all scaling at different speeds — classic Team of Teams territory.
prospects.company_overview: Ciena is a global networking systems and software company that provides optical and routing platforms for telecom carriers, data center operators, and enterprises. Headquartered in Hanover, Maryland, Ciena enables the world's largest networks to adapt to increasing bandwidth demand.

signals.description: CEO Calvin McDonald stepped down after 7-year tenure; CFO Meghan Frank serving as interim co-CEO while board conducts search for transformation-experienced leader
Shifting from product-led organizational structure to flatter, integrated model after CPO departure; 'Power of Three x2' growth strategy ($12.5B target) at risk as US sales stagnant for 7 quarters
Elliott Management took $1B activist stake amid leadership crisis; share price down 45% over prior year; pressure to restructure operations and revive North American growth

prospects.summary: Lululemon is at a crossroads — CEO departure, activist pressure from Elliott Management ($1B stake), a failed growth strategy, and a 45% stock decline. The incoming CEO will need to realign 40,000 employees around a new strategy while the org model is mid-transformation. Slightly above ICP revenue range at $11B, but the leadership vacuum and organizational complexity make this a compelling case for McChrystal's leadership and transformation capabilities.
prospects.entry_point: Marti Morfitt, Executive Chair — she's the continuity through the CEO transition with 18 years on the board. Susan Gelinas (Chief People and Culture Officer) as internal champion for organizational alignment work.
prospects.conversation_hook: Lululemon has an interim co-CEO structure, Elliott Management pushing their CEO candidate, Chip Wilson running a proxy fight, and 40,000 employees in limbo — whoever becomes CEO will need day-one organizational alignment, and the groundwork needs to start now.
prospects.company_overview: Lululemon is a global athletic apparel company known for premium yoga and fitness wear. Founded in Vancouver, the company operates 700+ stores worldwide and has expanded into footwear, accessories, and men's apparel while building a devoted community-driven brand.

signals.description: Jim Reagan appointed CEO on Feb 17, 2026 after serving as interim CEO since Oct 2025; predecessor Toni Townes-Whitley departed abruptly in Oct 2025

Collapsed five business units into three groups effective Jan 31, 2026 — Army/Navy combined, Air Force merged with Space & Intelligence, Civilian unchanged; wave of senior executive departures including two EVPs and Chief Innovation Officer
Lowered FY2026 revenue guidance from $7.6-7.75B to $7.25-7.325B in September 2025

prospects.summary: SAIC is a textbook McChrystal Group prospect — a $7.25B defense/IT services firm with 24,000 employees in the middle of a leadership transition and organizational overhaul. The new CEO is restructuring five units into three while dealing with senior executive departures and lowered revenue guidance. Defense sector expertise, cross-functional alignment after a reorg, and leadership development for a new executive team are all core McChrystal strengths.
prospects.entry_point: Jim Reagan, CEO (appointed Feb 2026) — former Leidos CFO who understands defense sector transformation. Prabu Natarajan (CFO) as secondary for financial framing of engagement ROI.
prospects.conversation_hook: SAIC collapsed five business units into three while losing multiple senior executives including the CEO, CIO, and two EVPs — new CEO Jim Reagan needs to build cohesion across a restructured 24,000-person org while executing $23.8B in backlog.

signals.description: 'Win as One' strategy overhauling entire operating model by mid-2026 — creating Chief Growth Officer, Chief Transformation Officer roles; reorganizing into six Operating Units from previous regional structure

Splitting EMEA & LatAm into three separate units (Latin America, Middle East/Africa, Europe); establishing India Subcontinent as standalone unit; expanding Executive Committee with new CGO, CTO, and Operating Unit Presidents

prospects.summary: Haleon, spun off from GSK in 2022, is executing a company-wide operating model transformation under its 'Win as One' strategy. The restructuring creates new C-suite roles (CGO, CTO), breaks up regional structures into six operating units, and expands the Executive Committee — exactly the kind of cross-functional reorganization where McChrystal Group's Team of Teams approach drives alignment. Revenue ($14.5B) is above the ICP sweet spot but the healthcare sector and transformation scale are a strong fit.
prospects.entry_point: Chief Transformation Officer (newly created role) — explicitly chartered to overhaul the operating model. Chief Growth Officer as secondary, also a new role created under the 'Win as One' strategy.
prospects.conversation_hook: Haleon is executing a company-wide 'Win as One' operating model overhaul by mid-2026 — creating two new C-suite roles, splitting EMEA into three units, and expanding the Executive Committee. The restructuring is happening right now.
prospects.company_overview: Haleon is the world's largest standalone consumer health company, spun off from GSK in 2022. The company owns iconic brands including Sensodyne, Advil, Voltaren, Centrum, and Theraflu, serving consumers across oral health, pain relief, vitamins, and respiratory health globally.

signals.description: Laying off ~4,000 employees (40% of workforce), reducing from 10,000 to ~6,000; $450-500M restructuring charge; one of the largest AI-related workforce reductions in fintech
CEO Jack Dorsey declared 'AI-first' operational strategy — shifting routine tasks to AI, consolidating teams, fundamentally reshaping how the company operates around Square and Cash App

prospects.summary: Block is undergoing one of the most dramatic corporate transformations of 2026 — cutting 40% of its workforce to reorganize around AI. While revenue ($24B) is above the ICP sweet spot, the remaining 6,000 employees face a massive cultural and operational shift. The challenge of rebuilding organizational identity, maintaining morale, and executing an 'AI-first' strategy with half the workforce is where leadership and team alignment consulting adds enormous value.
prospects.entry_point: Jack Dorsey, CEO — personally declared the 'AI-first' strategy driving the 40% workforce reduction. Head of People/HR as champion for the culture rebuild with the remaining 6,000 employees.
prospects.conversation_hook: Block just cut 40% of its workforce to go 'AI-first' — the remaining 6,000 employees face a complete identity and operational reset. Rebuilding organizational coherence after cutting half your people is a leadership problem, not a technology problem.

signals.description: Silver Lake completed $25B go-private acquisition of Endeavor in March 2025. Company rebranded to WME Group with complete leadership restructuring.
Founder Ari Emanuel stepped down as CEO, becoming executive chairman. Mark Shapiro elevated to president. Patrick Whitesell launched new Silver Lake-backed platform.
Massive asset divestiture: sold OpenBet, transferred IMG/PBR/On Location to TKO Group ($3.25B), Emanuel's MARI acquired IMG Arts & Entertainment. Entire organizational identity being rebuilt.

Transitioning from founder-led public conglomerate to PE-owned, focused entertainment platform. Rebuilding organizational identity, culture, and operating model after stripping away major business units.

prospects.summary: WME Group is one of the most dramatic corporate transformations happening right now: $25B go-private, founder departure, complete rebrand, massive divestitures, and new operating model — all in 12 months. Why this is worth the unconventional bet: entertainment is relationship-driven and resists corporate restructuring, making this exactly the kind of culture-first transformation McChrystal excels at.
prospects.entry_point: President & Managing Partner Mark Shapiro — he's the operational leader building the new WME Group. Silver Lake's deal lead would also be receptive given the integration complexity.
prospects.conversation_hook: Stripping a company down to its core after a $25B go-private is the easy part. Rebuilding culture and operating model with a workforce that watched their founder leave and half their colleagues get divested — that's happening now.
prospects.company_overview: WME Group (formerly Endeavor) is a global entertainment, sports, and content company that houses the William Morris Endeavor talent agency and holds a 61% stake in TKO Group (UFC + WWE). Taken private by Silver Lake in a $25B deal in March 2025, the company rebranded from Endeavor and underwent a complete leadership transition — founder Ari Emanuel moved to executive chairman while Mark Shapiro became president and managing partner.

signals.description: Acquired 51 healthcare operations in 2025 alone, bringing portfolio to 378 operations across 17 states. Revenue grew to $5.06B with guidance of $5.8B for 2026.
Aggressive acquisition strategy adding 1,200+ skilled nursing beds and 100+ senior living units per quarter. Building-by-building transition process across diverse geographies.
Scaling a decentralized 'servant leadership' model across 378 operations — each facility operates semi-autonomously, requiring a leadership pipeline producing dozens of new facility leaders per year.

prospects.summary: Ensign's decentralized operating model is remarkably similar to McChrystal's Team of Teams — semi-autonomous facilities connected by shared standards and culture. The challenge: adding 50+ facilities per year means they need to produce leaders faster than they're acquiring buildings.
prospects.entry_point: CEO Barry Port or COO — the executives managing the balance between acquisition pace and leadership pipeline. A board-level conversation about sustainable scaling would resonate.
prospects.conversation_hook: Ensign acquired 51 facilities in 2025. The question isn't whether the deal team can find targets — it's whether the leadership pipeline can produce enough qualified operators to run them without diluting the culture.
prospects.company_overview: The Ensign Group is one of the largest operators of skilled nursing facilities and senior living communities in the United States, with 378 healthcare operations across 17 states. Known for its decentralized 'servant leadership' operating model, Ensign empowers each facility to operate semi-autonomously while sharing resources and best practices through its central platform. The company acquired 51 facilities in 2025 alone and generated $5.06B in revenue.

signals.description: New CEO Patrick Kelleher appointed August 2025 (replacing retiring Malcolm Wilson), bringing 33 years of DHL experience. First COO (Bart Beeks) appointed January 2026.
Major organizational restructuring: new President of Americas/APAC from Ferguson, new COO role created, Chief Revenue Officer departed March 2026. Regional leaders now report directly to CEO.
150,000+ employees across 1,000+ facilities globally — new leadership team redesigning entire organizational structure to 'accelerate growth' post-spinoff from XPO.

prospects.summary: GXO is in the middle of a wholesale leadership and organizational overhaul: new CEO, new COO, new regional president, departing CRO, and flattened reporting lines — all while managing 150,000 employees across 1,000+ warehouses. This is exactly the kind of complex, high-stakes organizational transformation where McChrystal's operating model thrives.
prospects.entry_point: CEO Patrick Kelleher — he's actively reshaping the organization and would understand the value of a structured transformation framework. Alternatively, the new COO Bart Beeks, who owns operational excellence.
prospects.conversation_hook: New CEOs who restructure leadership teams within their first 6 months are signaling that the old operating model is broken. With 150,000 employees, GXO can't afford to get the new model wrong — and the window to set it right is closing.
prospects.company_overview: GXO Logistics is the world's largest pure-play contract logistics company, operating 1,000+ warehouses across 27 countries with approximately 150,000 employees. Spun off from XPO in 2021, GXO provides technology-driven supply chain solutions for Fortune 500 companies across e-commerce, consumer goods, healthcare, and industrial sectors. The company completed its acquisition of UK-based Wincanton in 2025.

signals.description: KKR-backed dental roll-up operating 1,900+ dental offices across 38 states — one of the largest DSOs in the U.S.
Navigating tension between standardized PE-driven operations and clinical autonomy dentists expect. Must maintain culture across 1,900+ locations with high provider turnover.
Rapid executive turnover and operational complexity as company integrates newly acquired practices while managing existing ones under PE performance pressure.

prospects.summary: Heartland Dental faces the signature PE roll-up dilemma at massive scale: 1,900+ locations, thousands of dentists who resist corporatization, and KKR-driven performance expectations. McChrystal's Team of Teams and culture change capabilities directly address the tension between central standardization and local clinical autonomy.
prospects.entry_point: Chief Operating Officer or Chief People Officer — the leader responsible for operational consistency and culture across 1,900+ offices. KKR's operating team could also champion an engagement.
prospects.conversation_hook: At 1,900+ locations, Heartland is essentially running a distributed military operation where every 'unit commander' (dentist) expects autonomy. The organizations that scale past this threshold without a deliberate operating model either stall or fracture.
prospects.company_overview: Heartland Dental is the largest dental support organization (DSO) in the United States, providing non-clinical business support to 1,900+ dental offices across 38 states. Backed by KKR, the company handles operations, marketing, procurement, and HR so dentists can focus on clinical care. Heartland recently launched Orahh Care Dental Community as a unified brand network across its supported practices.

signals.description: Completed transformational merger with Jacobs Solutions' Critical Mission Solutions and Cyber & Intelligence businesses in September 2024 — creating a $14.4B government services giant.
Multi-year integration of two massive organizations (53,000+ employees across 80 countries). Deleveraging from 4.0x to 3.2x while building unified culture and operating model.
Newly public company (NYSE: AMTM) navigating post-merger identity, organizational design, and government contract portfolio rationalization amid federal spending uncertainty.

prospects.summary: Amentum is the result of one of the largest government services mergers in recent history — combining two deeply technical, mission-critical organizations into one entity. CEO John Heller is a West Point graduate (natural McChrystal affinity). The challenge: unifying 53,000 people across 80 countries while maintaining classified program performance.
prospects.entry_point: CEO John Heller (West Point graduate) — he personally understands McChrystal's leadership philosophy. Alternatively, the CHRO or Chief Integration Officer managing the Jacobs merger integration.
prospects.conversation_hook: Merging two organizations that operate in classified environments across 80 countries isn't a standard integration — it's the kind of complexity that broke lesser organizations. McChrystal's team built the operating model for exactly this kind of distributed, high-stakes coordination.
prospects.company_overview: Amentum is a global leader in advanced engineering, technology, and mission support services for defense, intelligence, space, and civilian government markets. Formed through the 2024 merger of legacy Amentum with Jacobs Solutions' Critical Mission Solutions and Cyber & Intelligence businesses, the company now employs 53,000+ people across 80+ countries. Publicly traded on NYSE as AMTM, Amentum generated $14.4B in revenue in FY2025.

signals.description: Hellman & Friedman-backed collision repair roll-up with 1,700+ locations nationwide. Filed for IPO in 2024, signaling a potential public market transition.
Grew from regional player to 30,000+ employees and $7.5B revenue through hundreds of acquisitions, creating massive integration and culture alignment challenges.
IPO preparation requires shift from PE-backed growth-at-all-costs to public-company governance, operational transparency, and sustainable leadership pipeline.

prospects.summary: Caliber faces a dual challenge: integrating 1,700+ acquired collision repair shops under a unified culture while simultaneously preparing for public-market scrutiny. The PE-to-IPO transition demands the kind of organizational maturity, leadership development, and cross-functional alignment McChrystal delivers.
prospects.entry_point: CEO or Chief Transformation Officer — the executive leading IPO readiness. The Hellman & Friedman board representative responsible for value creation would also be receptive.
prospects.conversation_hook: Going public exposes every organizational crack. Caliber's 1,700+ locations need to operate like one company, not 1,700 acquisitions — and that transformation needs to happen before the IPO roadshow, not after.
prospects.company_overview: Caliber Collision is the nation's largest auto collision repair company, operating 1,700+ repair centers across 41 states with approximately 30,000 employees. Backed by Hellman & Friedman, the company was formed through the 2019 merger of Caliber and ABRA Auto Body and has continued to grow through hundreds of acquisitions. Caliber filed for an IPO in 2024, signaling a transition from PE-backed growth to public-company operations.

signals.description: Completed strategic merger with TruckPro (October 2025) to form largest independent heavy-duty aftermarket distributor in North America — 450+ locations, 110+ service centers.
New CEO Tom Greco appointed July 2025 to lead combined entity. Dual PE ownership (American Securities + Platinum Equity) adds governance complexity.
Merger resolved significant debt burden flagged by Moody's. Now must integrate two cultures, rationalize 450+ locations, and build unified operating model under new leadership.

prospects.summary: FleetPride is a freshly merged entity with a new CEO, two PE owners, and 450+ locations to integrate. The dual-sponsor PE structure adds complexity that most companies don't face. McChrystal's cross-functional alignment and leadership development capabilities directly address the integration challenge.
prospects.entry_point: CEO Tom Greco — hired specifically to lead post-merger integration. American Securities and Platinum Equity operating partners would also be receptive to organizational advisory.
prospects.conversation_hook: FleetPride has two PE owners, a new CEO, a freshly merged workforce, and 450+ locations that just became one company on paper. Making them one company in practice is the hard part — and the integration window is 12-18 months.
prospects.company_overview: FleetPride is the largest independent distributor of heavy-duty truck and trailer parts in North America, operating 450+ locations and 110+ service centers. Formed through the October 2025 merger of FleetPride and TruckPro under dual PE ownership (American Securities and Platinum Equity), the combined company serves commercial fleets, independent repair shops, and dealerships with parts, service, and technical support.

signals.description: Cut 150+ positions in Digital Wealth division in March 2026 as part of AI-driven restructuring. CEO Jeff Yabuki cited AI productivity gains and shift to productized innovation.
Motive Partners PE-backed company undergoing fundamental business model transformation from custom solutions to productized AI-first platform.
CEO Jeff Yabuki (former Fiserv CEO) leading a strategic pivot requiring complete organizational realignment — new operating model, new team structures, new skill requirements.

prospects.summary: InvestCloud is in the middle of a PE-imposed strategic pivot: cutting staff, shifting from custom to productized offerings, and rebuilding around AI. This is a classic 'transformation under pressure' scenario where McChrystal's organizational redesign and leadership alignment capabilities are directly applicable.
prospects.entry_point: CEO Jeff Yabuki — former Fiserv CEO who understands large-scale organizational transformation. He's the decision-maker driving this pivot and would value a structured approach.
prospects.conversation_hook: Layoffs get you to a smaller org chart — but they don't give you a better operating model. InvestCloud's AI pivot requires a fundamentally different way of working, not just fewer people doing the same things.
prospects.company_overview: InvestCloud is a global financial technology company that provides digital wealth management and financial planning platforms to over 500 financial institutions worldwide. Backed by Motive Partners, the company was formed through the 2021 merger of InvestCloud and Tegra118. Under CEO Jeff Yabuki (former Fiserv CEO), InvestCloud is pivoting from custom implementations to a productized, AI-first platform model.

signals.description: New CEO David Stevens effective January 2026, replacing founding CEO Milton Boyer. Also appointed new CFO (Daniel Speciale) and CIO (Prakash Pandian) — near-complete C-suite turnover.

Spun off from 3M and acquired by Altaris Capital Partners for $650M. Transitioning from a conglomerate division to standalone PE-backed CDMO — building independent identity, culture, and operating model.
Building new HQ in Woodbury, MN. Digital transformation initiative with new CIO. Succession plan initiated October 2025, completed January 2026.

prospects.summary: Kindeva is navigating one of the hardest transitions in business: from a protected division inside 3M to a standalone PE-backed company. Near-complete C-suite turnover, new HQ, digital transformation, and Altaris performance expectations create urgent need for leadership alignment and organizational design.
prospects.entry_point: New CEO David Stevens — he inherited a company mid-transformation and needs to build his leadership team's operating rhythm. Altaris Capital's deal partner would also be a strong champion.
prospects.conversation_hook: Spinoffs that replace the CEO, CFO, and CIO within 12 months are either building something new or struggling to hold together the old. Kindeva's window to set the cultural foundation is now.
prospects.company_overview: Kindeva Drug Delivery is a contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO) specializing in complex drug delivery systems, including inhalers, auto-injectors, and transdermal patches. Spun off from 3M and acquired by Altaris Capital Partners for $650M in 2020, the company has since acquired Meridian Medical Technologies (from Pfizer), iPharma Labs, and Summit Biosciences. Kindeva operates 10 facilities across the U.S. and U.K.

signals.description: PE-backed roll-up by Alpine Investors with 107+ acquisitions across HVAC, plumbing, and electrical services — integration of fragmented local brands into a single operating platform.
Scaled from startup to $2.2B revenue and 8,000+ employees through aggressive acquisition cadence, adding new companies monthly.
Facing classic roll-up integration challenge: standardizing culture, leadership, and operations across 107+ formerly independent companies while preserving local market relationships.

prospects.summary: Textbook McChrystal fit: PE-imposed timelines, 107+ acquired companies needing cultural unification, leadership development across dozens of local GMs, and cross-functional alignment between formerly independent operators. The 'Team of Teams' model is purpose-built for this kind of decentralized-but-connected challenge.
prospects.entry_point: COO or VP of Integration — the person responsible for post-acquisition integration across 107+ companies. Alpine Investors' operating partner overseeing Apex could also be a champion.
prospects.conversation_hook: With 107+ acquisitions and counting, Apex is past the point where ad-hoc integration works. The question isn't whether to systematize leadership and culture across the platform — it's whether they can do it fast enough to stay ahead of the acquisition pace.
prospects.company_overview: Apex Service Partners is one of the largest and fastest-growing home services platforms in the U.S., providing residential and commercial HVAC, plumbing, and electrical services. Backed by Alpine Investors, the company has acquired 107+ local service brands since its founding in 2019, building a nationwide network while preserving local brand identities in each market.

signals.description: 'Network of networks' pivot — Visa-as-a-Service platform transformation unveiled at Feb 2025 Investor Day, targeting $520B addressable market in Value-Added Services

Three major acquisitions requiring integration: Pismo ($929M, core banking infrastructure), Featurespace ($946M, AI fraud detection), and Prisma Medios de Pago (Argentina payments processor, Feb 2026)
1,400-person layoff in Jan 2025 across departments, AI-driven restructuring eliminating scrum masters and agile coaches, teams mandated to increase capacity 25% via AI
Ended 30-year NFL sponsorship (Mar 2026) to pivot to FIFA World Cup 2026 / LA28 Olympics; launched Trusted Agent Protocol with 10+ partners for agentic commerce (Oct 2025)

prospects.company_overview: Visa is the world's largest electronic payments network, processing over $15 trillion in total volume annually across 200+ countries. With $40B in FY2025 net revenue, Visa is undergoing a fundamental strategic transformation from a traditional card network into a 'network of networks' payments hyperscaler — building Visa-as-a-Service to capture a $520B addressable market in Value-Added Services. Under CEO Ryan McInerney (since Feb 2023), Visa is aggressively acquiring (Pismo $929M, Featurespace $946M, Prisma), restructuring its workforce (1,400-person layoff Jan 2025), and navigating a DOJ antitrust lawsuit that threatens its core debit business.
prospects.summary: Visa is attempting a once-in-a-generation platform transformation while simultaneously fighting an existential DOJ antitrust case, integrating 3 major acquisitions, and managing a demoralized workforce — a textbook VUCA environment requiring the cross-functional agility and shared consciousness that McChrystal Group delivers.
prospects.entry_point: Jack Forestell, Chief Product & Strategy Officer — owns the platform transformation vision and feels the cross-functional coordination pain daily as product, engineering, and acquired entities (Pismo, Featurespace) need to operate as a unified team.
prospects.conversation_hook: Visa's Jan 2025 layoff of 1,400 employees — including scrum masters and agile coaches — removed the connective tissue that enabled cross-team coordination, precisely when the 'network of networks' transformation demands more coordination, not less.

signals.description: Board approved separation into two independent public companies (Seed and Crop Protection)
Building two standalone operating models from single integrated company across 6 continents
CEO Chuck Magro moves to lead SpinCo (Seed); Chairman Greg Page leads New Corteva (Crop Protection) — entirely new leadership structures for each entity

prospects.company_overview: Corteva Agriscience is a global pure-play agriculture company spun off from DowDuPont in 2019, providing seed and crop protection products to farmers worldwide. The company operates through two segments — Seed (Pioneer, Brevant brands) and Crop Protection (herbicides, fungicides, insecticides, biologicals) — generating $17.7B in combined 2025 revenue. In October 2025, the board unanimously approved a plan to separate into two independent public companies by H2 2026.
prospects.summary: Corteva is executing one of the largest agricultural industry separations in history, splitting its $17.7B business into two standalone public companies — Seed (SpinCo, $9.9B) and Crop Protection (New Corteva, $7.8B). This creates enormous organizational design challenges: building duplicate leadership teams, separating shared AI/data platforms, disentangling global supply chains across 6 continents, and establishing two independent operating models from a single integrated culture. McChrystal's Team of Teams framework is directly applicable to the cross-functional coordination required during separation.
prospects.entry_point: Target the Chief Transformation Officer or SVP of Strategy leading the separation program management office — this person is navigating the most complex organizational design challenge in the company's history and needs external expertise in operating model design.
prospects.conversation_hook: Corteva's board approved the separation in October 2025 with a H2 2026 deadline — the company is now in the critical 12-month execution window where shared AI/data platform separation and duplicate leadership team buildout create the highest organizational risk.

In February 2026, QXO announced a $2.25B acquisition of Kodiak Building Partners (110 locations, $2.4B revenue). The company has stated a $50B revenue target within a decade.

The $50B revenue target means years of continuous M&A and integration at unprecedented speed. McChrystal's operating model is purpose-built for this challenge: creating shared consciousness across hundreds of acquired branches while enabling decentralized execution at each location.
prospects.entry_point: Target the Chief Integration Officer or SVP of Operations responsible for harmonizing Beacon's 600-branch network with Kodiak's 110 locations — this person faces the most acute cross-functional integration challenge in building products distribution history.
prospects.conversation_hook: QXO announced the $2.25B Kodiak acquisition in February 2026 while still integrating Beacon's 600 branches from the April 2025 close — the company is now running two massive integrations simultaneously with a $50B revenue target creating relentless acquisition pressure.

prospects.company_overview: Grant Thornton is the seventh-largest accounting firm in the U.S. by revenue ($2.4B, FY2023 record). In May 2024, New Mountain Capital acquired a 60% stake — making Grant Thornton the largest U.S. accounting firm to take private equity investment. The firm now operates in an alternative practice structure: Grant Thornton LLP (attest services) and Grant Thornton Advisors LLC (business advisory/non-attest). CDPQ and OA Private Capital are minority investors alongside New Mountain.
prospects.summary: Grant Thornton is the first major accounting firm to undergo PE-backed transformation, with New Mountain Capital's 60% stake creating enormous pressure to accelerate growth, execute acquisitions, and modernize operations within PE hold-period timelines. The alternative practice structure (CPA firm + advisory LLC) creates a dual-governance challenge that requires cross-functional alignment. McChrystal's expertise in building shared consciousness across structurally separated but operationally intertwined organizations maps directly to this challenge.
prospects.entry_point: Target the Chief Operating Officer or Chief Transformation Officer responsible for executing the PE-backed growth strategy — this person must align a partnership-culture accounting firm with PE-speed execution requirements.
prospects.conversation_hook: Grant Thornton's New Mountain Capital deal closed in May 2024, giving the firm a war chest for acquisitions and technology investment — but transforming a partnership-culture professional services firm to PE-speed execution is an organizational design challenge that the accounting industry has never faced before.

The appointment of a Chief Transformation Officer in September 2025 signals the beginning of a major organizational overhaul — transforming siloed country-level operations into a coordinated global platform while integrating acquisitions (Bottomline LSM, others). McChrystal's cross-functional alignment and Team of Teams operating model is precisely what a CTO at a 33,000-person, 80-country operation needs to break down geographic silos.
prospects.entry_point: Target Vishy Padmanabhan, Chief Transformation Officer (appointed September 2025) — he is literally tasked with transforming a 33,000-person global operation and needs external expertise in cross-functional organizational design at scale.
prospects.conversation_hook: Sedgwick appointed Vishy Padmanabhan as Chief Transformation Officer in September 2025 — a newly created role signaling that Carlyle and the leadership team recognize the 33,000-person, 80-country operation needs a fundamental operating model redesign, not just incremental improvement.

dossiers.org_structure: Consolidated from 5 business groups to 3 (ANG, AFSI, Civilian) effective Jan 31, 2026. CTO role merged with Chief Growth Officer. New Chief Talent Officer hired.
dossiers.culture_signals: Glassdoor 3.9/5.0 (4,822 reviews); 79% recommend. Work-life balance 4.2. Named to Glassdoor 2026 Best Places to Work. Positives: flexible work, growth opportunities. Negatives: management concerns (workload, favoritism), contract-based job insecurity, bureaucratic processes. Gap between 'Best Places to Work' and management complaints suggests inconsistency across business units — hallmark of siloed organizations.

fit_assessment.primary_problem: 24,000-person defense/IT firm navigating simultaneous CEO transition, 5-to-3 group consolidation, post-acquisition integration (SilverEdge), and DOGE-driven market disruption. Needs to move faster, break silos, create shared consciousness across distributed workforce.
fit_assessment.best_capability_fit: Team of Teams — directly addresses 5-to-3 consolidation. Leadership development — new CEO, new group leaders, executive departures. Cross-functional alignment — CTO/CGO merger, talent officer hire, innovation office restructuring. Post-acquisition integration — SilverEdge (small, agile) into SAIC (large, matrixed).
likely_objections: We already have military DNA
Just reorganized — need to let changes settle
Budget pressure makes discretionary consulting tough
competitive_landscape: Booz Allen Hamilton — direct competitor in gov IT, unlikely hired as advisor
McKinsey/BCG — strategy/org design but lack military credibility
Korn Ferry — leadership assessment
McChrystal advantage: military heritage + instant credibility with board (two West Point graduates)
fit_assessment.rating_rationale: Textbook. Tier 1 industry, $7.5B revenue (sweet spot), 24K+ employees, active CEO transition, reorg, acquisition, DOGE disruption, board loaded with military leaders. Timing exceptional: new permanent CEO Feb 2026.

conversation_entries.opening_question: You've consolidated five groups into three and stated the goal is 'speed, flexibility, and efficiency.' The hardest part isn't the new org chart — it's getting the Army group and Navy group to actually operate as one team. How are you building shared consciousness across merged groups?
SilverEdge was a 200-person software shop built for agility. SAIC is 24,000 people optimizing for scale. What's your integration philosophy — protecting SilverEdge's DNA or assimilating them?

You both know from military service that the hardest transitions are combining units with different operational tempos. General McChrystal faced the same challenge getting Army, Navy, Air Force, and CIA operating as one force in Iraq. We've codified those lessons.
conversation_entries.framing: Direct at board members Tien or Urban

dossiers.recommended_meeting_framing: Position around 'operating model for the next chapter' — Reagan is building, not repairing. Frame as partner in designing how reorganized SAIC should operate. Lead with Team of Teams as proven model for military-to-corporate transformation. Avoid positioning as response to Townes-Whitley departure.

signals.description: 20% workforce reduction (~900 positions) by end of 2027; 200 positions to Houston; 360 offshored
Consolidating corporate functions into ExxonMobil global centers; selling Calgary HQ; relocating to Edmonton

prospect.summary: Company-defining transformation — becoming less autonomous, more integrated with ExxonMobil while maintaining operational excellence.
prospect.entry_point: Dan Lyons (SVP Finance, U.S. Army veteran); John Whelan (CEO)
prospect.conversation_hook: SVP Finance Dan Lyons is a U.S. Army veteran — direct McChrystal connection
prospect.company_overview: Imperial Oil is one of Canada's largest integrated oil companies, with operations spanning oil sands production, conventional crude oil, petroleum refining, and fuel marketing. Majority-owned by ExxonMobil, the company operates the Kearl oil sands mine and Strathcona refinery.

dossiers.headquarters: Calgary, Alberta, Canada (relocating operations to Edmonton by 2028)
dossiers.ownership_structure: Public (TSX/NYSE: IMO). ExxonMobil holds 69.6% majority stake.
dossiers.geographic_footprint: Upstream: Kearl oil sands, Cold Lake. Downstream: Strathcona Refinery (Edmonton), Sarnia, Nanticoke. Esso retail network across Canada. Corporate functions migrating to Houston and ExxonMobil centers in India, Argentina, Thailand, Brazil.
dossiers.financial_health: Revenue: FY2024 ~CA$51.5B. Net income CA$4.79B. Cash from operations CA$5.98B. Record production 438,000 boe/d (highest in 30+ years). Q4 2025: EPS beat by 1.55% but revenue missed by 10.41%. Dividend raised 20% in Q1 2026. Analyst sentiment: Hold. TD Securities: Sell. BMO: downgraded to Market Perform. Restructuring targeting $150M annual savings by 2028.

key_contacts.name: Dan Lyons
key_contacts.title: SVP, Finance & Administration
key_contacts.background: U.S. Army veteran. BA Economics Vassar; MBA Chicago. Joined ExxonMobil 1990. Career across Dallas, Bangkok, Houston, Saudi Arabia, London.
key_contacts.priority_rationale: U.S. Army veteran — direct connection to McChrystal Group's military network. As finance leader, controls consulting budgets. Deep institutional knowledge. Entry angle: veteran-to-veteran conversation.

key_contacts.name: John Whelan
key_contacts.title: Chairman, President & CEO
key_contacts.background: From St. John's, Newfoundland. 37-year ExxonMobil career. Previously SVP Upstream at ExxonMobil (13 global business units). Memorial University B.Eng.
key_contacts.priority_rationale: New CEO executing most significant transformation in Imperial's modern history within first year.

key_contacts.name: Cheryl Gomez-Smith
key_contacts.title: SVP, Upstream
key_contacts.background: BS Mech. Eng. Notre Dame; MBA Tulane

key_contacts.name: Scott Maloney
key_contacts.title: VP, Downstream
key_contacts.background: 12+ years in ExxonMobil lubricants, retail fuel. Moved to Calgary 2023.

dossiers.org_structure: Classic integrated oil company with upstream/downstream/corporate divisions. Historically operated with meaningful autonomy from ExxonMobil ('corporate separateness'). The 2025 restructuring collapses that autonomy — centralizing into ExxonMobil's global centers.
dossiers.culture_signals: Glassdoor 3.3/5.0; Culture & Values 2.9/5 (lowest); only 47% recommend. Indeed 4.0/5. Positives: good people, strong pay, learning opportunities. Negatives: 'slow company,' excessive processes, bureaucracy. [INFERRED] Post-restructuring morale likely significantly worse. Key challenges: identity crisis (becoming ExxonMobil branch vs. autonomous Canadian company), survivor syndrome, knowledge drain (900 departures), two-speed org (operations untouched, corporate gutted), political headwind (Alberta Premier and federal minister publicly criticized).

trigger_events.event: CEO Brad Corson announces retirement; John Whelan named successor
trigger_events.relevance: New CEO inheriting and executing biggest restructuring. Classic 'new leader, big mandate' moment.

trigger_events.event: 20% workforce reduction (~900 jobs), offshoring, Houston migration announced
trigger_events.relevance: Primary trigger. Massive transformation with high execution risk. Team of Teams built for maintaining cohesion during structural change.

trigger_events.event: Alberta Premier and Federal Energy Minister publicly criticize restructuring
trigger_events.relevance: Political backlash adds reputational risk. Must manage external stakeholders while executing internally.

trigger_events.event: Imperial signs deal to sell Quarry Park campus (800,000 sq ft)
trigger_events.relevance: Physical symbol of transformation. Losing HQ = identity-defining moment.

trigger_events.event: Record annual production (438,000 boe/d) despite restructuring
trigger_events.relevance: Operationally strong but restructuring from strength can make alignment harder — 'why fix what's not broken?'

trigger_events.event: 2026 guidance: 441-460K boe/d, $2.0-2.2B capex, 20% dividend increase
trigger_events.relevance: Shareholder-returns narrative dominant. Risk: workforce perceives leadership prioritizing shareholders over employees.

fit_assessment.primary_problem: Imperial Oil is eliminating 20% of its workforce, offshoring critical functions, relocating HQ, and fundamentally restructuring its relationship with parent ExxonMobil — under a new CEO, with political opposition, in an industry facing long-term transition pressure. Risk is organizational: maintaining excellence, knowledge, collaboration, and commitment during 2+ years of sustained disruption.
fit_assessment.best_capability_fit: Team of Teams — fragmenting into geographically dispersed nodes (Edmonton, Houston, India, Argentina, Thailand, Brazil). Cross-functional alignment — upstream/downstream/corporate silos being physically separated. Leadership development — new CEO, new downstream VP. Culture change — trust destroyed by layoffs/offshoring.
likely_objections: ExxonMobil manages our consulting relationships
Internal change management capabilities via ExxonMobil
Restructuring plan is set — just need to execute
Budget pressure — entire point is cost reduction
competitive_landscape: McKinsey — likely involved (ExxonMobil is known McKinsey client) [INFERRED]
Korn Ferry — strong O&G org consulting practice
Deloitte — major Calgary energy sector presence
Internal ExxonMobil resources — biggest competitor
fit_assessment.rating_rationale: Textbook opportunity. New CEO, massive workforce reduction, geographic dispersion, identity transformation, political headwinds, high-stakes operating environment. Dan Lyons military connection provides warm entry. Primary risk: ExxonMobil controlling consulting relationship from Houston.

conversation_entries.opening_question: You're asking 80% of your people to stay committed while watching colleagues lose their jobs and seeing the company's identity change. How are you maintaining shared purpose across a workforce now spread from Edmonton to Houston to Mumbai?

Dan, you served in the Army — you know what it takes to maintain unit cohesion during reorganization. How is Imperial preserving the knowledge and relationships that keep Kearl and Cold Lake running at record levels while the corporate structure changes?
conversation_entries.framing: Veteran-to-veteran framing with Dan Lyons

conversation_entries.opening_question: The political reaction from Alberta and Ottawa was unusually sharp. How is leadership managing the external narrative while keeping internal execution on track?

dossiers.recommended_meeting_framing: Enter through Dan Lyons (veteran connection, economic buyer) with goal of earning a meeting with Whelan. Frame as 'operational risk mitigation, knowledge transfer velocity, and cross-functional execution speed' — avoid 'culture' language (ExxonMobil culture is engineering-driven and metrics-focused).

signals.description: Emerged from Chapter 11 Sept 29, 2025; eliminated $4.6B debt; reduced workforce 20%
Transitioning 150mm to 200mm SiC; closed Durham fab; consolidating to Mohawk Valley mega-fab

prospect.summary: Post-bankruptcy rebuilding with near-total leadership turnover, manufacturing consolidation, and workforce trauma.
prospect.entry_point: Robert Feurle (CEO); Anthony Abate (Board Chairman, former U.S. Air Force officer)
prospect.conversation_hook: Board Chairman Abate is former Air Force — natural McChrystal affinity
prospect.company_overview: Wolfspeed is a global leader in silicon carbide (SiC) semiconductor technology, producing power devices and materials critical to electric vehicles, renewable energy, and 5G infrastructure. Spun off from Cree, the company is investing $6.5B+ to build next-generation manufacturing capacity.

dossiers.ownership_structure: Public (NYSE: WOLF). Post-bankruptcy ownership: former senior secured noteholders (Apollo, Baupost, Fidelity, Capital Group). Renesas converted ~$2B supply deposit into equity/warrants.
dossiers.geographic_footprint: Mohawk Valley Fab (Marcy, NY) — primary 200mm production. John Palmour Mfg Center (Siler City, NC) — materials/crystal growth. Durham, NC — corporate HQ and R&D. Germany fab plans abandoned.
dossiers.financial_health: Revenue: FY2024 $807M → FY2025 $758M (-6%). Q2 FY2026 $168M quarterly. GAAP net loss $469M FY2025. Mohawk Valley underutilization costs ~$47M/quarter. Post-restructuring: ~$2B debt (down from $4.6B), $1.3B cash. Received $698.6M CHIPS Act tax refunds. $750M CHIPS Act grant preliminary but uncertain. Analyst sentiment: Mixed/cautious. Target: positive unlevered operating cash flow by end FY2026.

key_contacts.priority_rationale: New CEO (10 months in) leading post-bankruptcy transformation with 20% reduced workforce. Must rebuild trust, align demoralized org around new mission.

key_contacts.priority_rationale: Air Force veteran — natural affinity for McChrystal. Creditor-appointed chairman with mandate to ensure turnaround delivers. Internal champion/validator.

key_contacts.name: Gregor van Issum
key_contacts.title: CFO
key_contacts.background: 20+ years transformation and strategic financing. Previously Chief Transformation Officer at ams-OSRAM; VP Strategy at NXP. Explicitly hired as 'Transformation and Strategic Financing Expert.'

key_contacts.name: Cengiz Balkas

dossiers.org_structure: Transitioning from multi-site R&D-oriented org (legacy Cree, university spinout) to focused high-volume manufacturer running single mega-fab. Near-total leadership turnover: new CEO, CFO, COO, board chairman all within 12 months. COO role newly created June 2025.
dossiers.culture_signals: Glassdoor 3.2/5.0 (934 reviews) — below semiconductor avg. Positives: safety culture, state-of-art tech, good benefits. Negatives: 'Disconnect between upper management and those who get work done is breathtaking'; lack of ownership and transparency; frequent team structure changes; still rooted in R&D culture vs. high-volume manufacturing mindset; 'huge employee turnover.' CFO explicitly hired as 'Transformation Expert' — company acknowledges it is in transformation mode.

trigger_events.event: CEO Gregg Lowe ousted after 7 years; board chairman named Executive Chairman
trigger_events.relevance: Leadership crisis. Organization lost primary strategic leader amid existential financial pressure.

trigger_events.event: 20% workforce reduction (~1,000+ positions); Durham fab closure; Germany expansion abandoned
trigger_events.relevance: Massive organizational trauma. Surviving employees face uncertainty and broken trust.

trigger_events.event: Robert Feurle appointed CEO; Dr. David Emerson joins as first-ever COO (June 2025)
trigger_events.relevance: New leader inheriting demoralized, restructured org. Must build trust and alignment fast.

trigger_events.event: Chapter 11 emergence after 91-day prepackaged restructuring; $4.6B debt eliminated; entire board reconstituted
trigger_events.relevance: Post-bankruptcy identity reset. New ownership, board, mandate. Must redefine culture and operating model.

trigger_events.event: Gregor van Issum appointed CFO — explicitly as 'transformation specialist'
trigger_events.relevance: Company views itself as in active transformation, not just financial recovery.

trigger_events.event: Q2 FY2026 disappoints; guidance below expectations; additional headcount reduction
trigger_events.relevance: Continued pressure means transformation must deliver quickly. No room for slow cultural evolution.

trigger_events.event: Launched industry-first 10kV SiC MOSFET; unveiled 300mm SiC platform for AI data centers
trigger_events.relevance: Strategic ambition exists but executing requires organizational alignment that may not yet exist.

fit_assessment.primary_problem: Multi-dimensional transformation: new leadership team (CEO, CFO, COO, board all replaced in 12 months), post-bankruptcy rebuilding, manufacturing transition (150mm to 200mm, multi-site to mega-fab), cultural shift (R&D to high-volume manufacturing), and strategic pivot (EV-centric to broader markets incl. AI). Workforce has been through layoffs, bankruptcy, CEO firing, and facility closures.
fit_assessment.best_capability_fit: Team of Teams — breaking silos between Mohawk Valley manufacturing, Siler City materials, sales, and R&D. Leadership development — entirely new C-suite needs rapid trust and shared consciousness. Crisis leadership — CHIPS Act uncertainty, EV demand softness, competitive pressure = VUCA. Culture change — R&D/academic culture (Cree legacy) to disciplined manufacturing execution.
likely_objections: Just went through bankruptcy — can't afford premium consulting
New leadership needs time to settle
Semiconductor company — military consulting not relevant
CFO was already a Chief Transformation Officer
competitive_landscape: McKinsey — dedicated semiconductor practice
Korn Ferry — leadership assessment / C-suite onboarding
AlixPartners/Alvarez & Marsal — may already be involved (bankruptcy context)
McChrystal differentiator: mission-driven Team of Teams model for post-bankruptcy cultural alignment. Chairman Abate's Air Force background = natural entry.
fit_assessment.rating_rationale: High-conviction. Post-bankruptcy rebuilding, near-total leadership turnover, manufacturing consolidation, workforce trauma, VUCA environment. Former Air Force officer as board chairman provides natural entry point.

conversation_entries.opening_question: You've replaced nearly every senior leader and board member in 12 months while consolidating from multiple facilities to a single mega-fab. How are you building shared consciousness and trust across teams operating in fundamentally new ways?

Glassdoor and your own hiring patterns suggest Wolfspeed is transitioning from R&D culture to high-volume manufacturing culture. In our experience, that's harder than the technology transition itself. How is that going internally?

Your board chairman started his career as an Air Force officer. Our founder built the operating model that transformed Joint Special Operations Command. There may be a shared philosophy about high-performing teams under pressure. Worth exploring?
conversation_entries.framing: Leverage Abate's military background

dossiers.recommended_meeting_framing: Position around 'post-restructuring organizational alignment.' Frame: 'Organizations that emerge from Chapter 11 with new leadership and reduced workforce have a 12-18 month window to establish operating norms that determine success. Most fail not because strategy is wrong but because the organization can't execute fast enough. We help close that gap.'

signals.description: 'Win as One' strategy: overhauling operating model by mid-2026; creating CGO and CTO roles; reorganizing into six Operating Units
Splitting EMEA & LatAm into three units; India Subcontinent standalone; expanding Executive Committee

prospect.summary: Company-wide operating model transformation under 'Win as One' strategy — creating six Operating Units from three regions.
prospect.entry_point: Brian McNamara (CEO); Jonathan Workman (Chief Transformation Officer)
prospect.conversation_hook: 'Win as One' strategy name itself signals collaboration/unity problem — directly maps to Team of Teams
prospect.company_overview: Haleon is the world's largest standalone consumer health company, spun off from GSK in 2022. The company owns iconic brands including Sensodyne, Advil, Voltaren, Centrum, and Theraflu, serving consumers across oral health, pain relief, vitamins, and respiratory health globally.

dossiers.ownership_structure: Public — dual-listed London Stock Exchange (FTSE 100) and NYSE. GSK and Pfizer have fully exited. BlackRock is largest shareholder.
dossiers.geographic_footprint: 170+ markets. Reorganizing into six Operating Units: North America, Europe, Latin America, MEA, APAC, India Subcontinent. Completed full buyout of China OTC JV (TSKF) June 2025.
dossiers.financial_health: Revenue: FY2024 GBP 11.2B → FY2025 GBP 11.0B (organic +3.0%, below 4-6% guidance). Adj. operating profit +10.5% organically. Gross margins +220 bps. FY2026 guidance: organic 3-5%, high single-digit adj. operating profit growth. GBP 800M productivity target over 5 years. Consensus Moderate Buy (9 buy, 1 hold, 1 sell). Target ~430p (~15% upside). Pressures: top-line growth below guidance, FX headwinds. Tailwinds: margin expansion, productivity savings, shareholder overhang cleared.

key_contacts.title: CEO
key_contacts.tenure: Since May 2022; at GSK Consumer Healthcare since 2015
key_contacts.background: BS Electrical Engineering (Union College), MBA Finance (Cincinnati). 16 years P&G, 10 years Novartis (Executive Committee). Mondelez board.
key_contacts.priority_rationale: Architect of 'Win as One' transformation. Publicly committed to overhauling entire operating model by mid-2026. Engineer-turned-CEO who values structured frameworks.

key_contacts.title: Chief Transformation Officer / President Europe
key_contacts.background: 15 years P&G, then 15+ years GSK/Haleon in GM roles across Asia, Africa, Europe. President of AESGP.
key_contacts.priority_rationale: Owns transformation execution. P&G discipline + diverse GM experience = understands complexity of making decentralized models work. Most likely internal champion.

key_contacts.name: Dawn Allen
key_contacts.title: CFO
key_contacts.background: 25 years at Mars (rose to Global CFO & VP Global Transformation). CFO Tate & Lyle. ICAEW member. Her Mars title 'VP Global Transformation' signals direct transformation experience.

key_contacts.name: Filippo Lanzi
key_contacts.title: Chief Growth Officer
key_contacts.tenure: Appointed Feb 2026; previously President EMEA & LatAm since 2021
key_contacts.background: 9 years Nestle, then J&J, GSK since 2015. Oversees Category, Marketing, Strategy, Commercial Excellence.

key_contacts.name: Vindi Banga
key_contacts.title: Chairman
key_contacts.tenure: Board since 2022 IPO; Chairman since Jan 2026
key_contacts.background: 33 years Unilever (CEO Hindustan Unilever). Operating Partner at Clayton Dubilier & Rice. Chair of UK Government Investments (UKGI). Chair Imperial College London Council.

dossiers.org_structure: Reorganizing from three geographic regions into six Operating Units with direct CEO reporting. Creating CGO and CTO to break functional silos. Eliminating CMO role. Category-led model replacing geographic fiefdoms.
dossiers.culture_signals: Glassdoor 3.6/5.0. Career opportunities 3.4 (weakness). 70% recommend. Concerns: 'lack of career progression,' 'no job security' during restructuring, some regions flagged as 'toxic.' Still shedding GSK pharma DNA while building consumer-first culture. Six Operating Units while asking people to 'Win as One' is an inherent tension. Multiple leadership changes (CFO, CHRO, CMO, Chairman, R&D Chief all changed 2024-2026) — restructuring fatigue risk.

trigger_events.event: FY2025 results: organic +3.0% (below guidance); announced operating model overhaul with CGO, CTO, six Operating Units
trigger_events.relevance: Publicly admitting current structure doesn't deliver growth. Overhauling entire operating model = maximum organizational disruption and advisory need.

trigger_events.event: Vindi Banga becomes Chairman, succeeding Sir Dave Lewis
trigger_events.relevance: New board leadership catalyzes fresh thinking. Banga's CD&R/Unilever background values operational transformation.

trigger_events.event: Pfizer sells final 7.3% stake for ~$3.3B. Haleon fully independent.
trigger_events.relevance: First time without former parent oversight. Can pursue own organizational identity.

trigger_events.event: Carl Haney appointed Chief R&D Officer (external hire)
trigger_events.relevance: Another senior change adding to team dynamics that need integration.

trigger_events.event: Completed full buyout of China OTC JV (TSKF)
trigger_events.relevance: Integrating previously semi-independent entity into new operating model. Adds complexity.

trigger_events.event: Capital Markets Day: 'Win as One' strategy, GBP 800M productivity target, 1B more consumers by 2030
trigger_events.relevance: Public commitment. CEO has staked reputation on this strategy delivering.

trigger_events.event: Dawn Allen joins as CFO from Mars/Tate & Lyle
trigger_events.relevance: Allen's Mars title 'VP Global Transformation' — hired for transformation credentials alongside financial expertise.

fit_assessment.primary_problem: Transforming a 24,000-person company's entire operating model — from regional geographic structure to category-led six-Operating-Unit model — in ~18 months, while delivering growth acceleration and GBP 175-200M cost savings. Challenge is organizational execution at speed: getting six new unit leaders, new CGO, CTO, CFO, CHRO, and R&D chief to operate cohesively while org chart redraws underneath them.
fit_assessment.best_capability_fit: Team of Teams — creating exactly the kind of decentralized multi-unit structure that fails without shared consciousness. Cross-functional alignment — CGO must coordinate Category, Marketing, Strategy, R&D, Supply Chain across six regions. Leadership development — multiple new Executive Committee members who have never worked together.
likely_objections: Already have a Chief Transformation Officer and detailed plan
Consumer healthcare, not military/defense
Focused on cost savings — can't add advisory spend
Already working with Big 4 / McKinsey [INFERRED]
competitive_landscape: McKinsey — likely already involved given size and scope
Korn Ferry — org structure / Executive Committee expansion
BCG/Bain — consumer goods sector expertise
Deloitte — technology/process transformation
McChrystal differentiator: 'Win as One' is literally Team of Teams. No competitor offers the operating model framework for making decentralized structures operate as one.
fit_assessment.rating_rationale: Transformation publicly committed and underway. Organizational challenge maps directly to core offering. CEO staked reputation on it. Timing right (mid-2026 deadline). Company large enough for premium advisory.

conversation_entries.opening_question: You've committed to making six Operating Units work as one team by mid-2026. What's your plan for building shared consciousness across unit leaders who have historically competed for resources?

Your new CGO role sits at the intersection of Category, Marketing, Strategy, R&D, Supply Chain, and six regional units. How are you ensuring Filippo Lanzi has the connective tissue to lead across all those interfaces without becoming a bottleneck?
With a new CFO, CHRO, CTO, R&D chief, and Chairman all joining in 18 months, your Executive Committee is essentially a startup team inside a GBP 11B company. How are you accelerating team formation?

dossiers.recommended_meeting_framing: Frame as 'lessons from organizations that have successfully decentralized at scale.' Bring case studies. Hook: 'The ones that succeed invest as much in the operating system — how teams connect and share information — as they do in the org chart. The ones that fail assume the new structure creates new behavior. It never does.'

signals.description: Q3 2025 revenue $743M (-14% YoY); adj. EBITDA fell 50%+ to $30M; FY2025 net loss ~$408-418M
Closing German polystyrene asset, repurposing Italian MMA facility; $30M+ annualized savings
Pivoting to recycled PMMA; $60-70M restructuring over three years; bankruptcy discussions active

prospect.summary: Company in acute financial distress fighting for survival while restructuring operations across borders and pivoting product strategy.
prospect.entry_point: Frank Bozich (CEO); Paula Cooney (CHRO)
prospect.conversation_hook: Imminent Chapter 11 creates urgent need for crisis leadership and organizational cohesion
prospect.company_overview: Trinseo is a specialty materials company producing plastics, latex binders, and synthetic rubber for automotive, construction, consumer electronics, and packaging markets. Formerly part of Dow Chemical, the company operates manufacturing sites across the Americas, Europe, and Asia.

dossiers.ownership_structure: Public (NYSE: TSE, trading as TSEOF after non-compliance). ~72% institutional. Incorporated in Ireland. Originally carved out from Dow Chemical by Bain Capital.
dossiers.geographic_footprint: 21 manufacturing sites across 4 continents: Europe (Germany, Belgium, France, Italy, Finland, Switzerland, Spain, Sweden), North America (PA, CT, GA, MI), Latin America (Brazil, Mexico), Asia-Pacific (China, Hong Kong, Taiwan, India, Japan, Korea). 11 R&D facilities.
dossiers.financial_health: Revenue: FY2024 $3.51B (-4.4% from FY2023). Q3 2025 $743M (-14% YoY). Net loss $110M in Q3. Long-term debt ~$2.33B. Shareholder equity deficit $(862M). Debt leverage ~13-15x. Market cap collapsed to $11.7M (down 90% in 12 months). S&P downgraded to SD (Selective Default) after missed interest payments in Feb/Mar 2026. Bankruptcy filing widely expected. Cash conservation paramount.

key_contacts.title: President & CEO
key_contacts.background: CEO of SI Group (2013-2019). Multiple BASF executive roles. BS Chemistry, MBA Chicago, MS Chemistry Illinois. Board of OGE Energy. $3.2M retention bonus Jan 2026.
key_contacts.priority_rationale: Decision-maker navigating existential crisis. Must maintain operational continuity across 21 plants on 4 continents while negotiating with creditors. Crisis leadership maps directly to McChrystal.

key_contacts.name: Paula Cooney

conversation_entries.opening_question: Frank, you've got 3,100 people across 21 plants on four continents watching bankruptcy headlines. How are you keeping the operators in Tessenderlo and Rho focused on running their lines while lawyers in New York negotiate with creditors?

You merged three business segments last October and you're closing plants in Germany while ramping recycled PMMA in Italy — are your regional leaders actually operating as one team, or still fighting the US-versus-Europe dynamic?
The retention bonuses signal your board wants this leadership team intact. But what about the next two layers down? Who's keeping your plant managers and technical talent from walking?

dossiers.recommended_meeting_framing: Do NOT lead with organizational consulting. Lead with crisis leadership experience. Frame: 'We help leadership teams operate as one when stakes are highest and margin for error is zero.' Position as confidential 45-minute conversation. Offer concrete 4-6 week 'crisis operating rhythm' assessment. Keep initial price modest ($150K-250K) to get in door.

prospect.company_overview: Block (formerly Square) is a global financial technology company founded by Jack Dorsey. The company operates Square (merchant payment processing), Cash App (consumer finance), TIDAL (music streaming), and Spiral (bitcoin development), serving millions of businesses and consumers.

dossiers.ownership_structure: Public (NYSE: XYZ). Added to S&P 500 July 2025.
dossiers.geographic_footprint: Global — US-centric with international presence (Square international GPV grew 24% Q4 2025). Portfolio: Square (merchant services), Cash App (consumer wallet), Afterpay (BNPL), TIDAL (music), Proto (Bitcoin mining), Bitkey (hardware wallet).
dossiers.financial_health: Revenue: FY2025 ~$24.2B; FY2026E ~$26.8B. Q4 2025: $7.4B revenue, $2.87B gross profit (+24% YoY). Record net income $1.1B in Q4. First time exceeding Rule of 40 (score of 44%). FY2026 guidance: 17% gross profit growth, adj. operating income $3.2B (26% margin), adj. EPS $3.66 (+54%). Restructuring charge $450-500M in Q1 2026. Consensus Buy (30 analysts). Stock surged ~24% on layoff/earnings day. This is NOT a distressed company — record profitability funds premium advisory.

dossiers.org_structure: Shifting from business-line GM model (Square, Cash App, etc. as independent fiefdoms) to functionally oriented structure. Describes itself as 'federation of companies.' AI mandates: every employee required to use generative AI daily with usage in performance evaluations.
dossiers.culture_signals: Glassdoor ~1,860 reviews; mixed sentiment. Positives: diversity, purpose-driven, innovative culture. Negatives: frequent restructuring (2023-2026 multiple rounds), difficulty completing projects due to constant team changes, heavy AI adoption pressure, high KPIs, leadership development gaps. One employee: 'worst I've felt in four years.' Data scientist publicly quit refusing 90% raise citing survivor's guilt. AI mandates described as 'surveillance-style.' Key challenge: survivor's guilt and trust erosion among remaining 6,000; forced tech adoption without cultural scaffolding; siloed-to-functional reorg.

fit_assessment.primary_problem: Cutting 40% of workforce, mandating AI fluency, restructuring from siloed business units to functional model — all while maintaining shipping velocity of a company twice its size. Financial results strong but human/organizational execution is where risk lives. Trust eroded, morale at multi-year lows, 'AI-first' narrative faces AI-washing accusations.
fit_assessment.best_capability_fit: Team of Teams — Block is literally trying to do more with fewer people by creating smaller, empowered, cross-functional teams. Organizational design — shift from business-line to functional alignment. Culture change / trust-building — rebuilding trust with 6,000 remaining employees who watched half their colleagues leave. Leadership development — Glassdoor explicitly cites maturation gap.

fit_assessment.rating_rationale: Multiple active trigger events, record profitability (can afford engagement), leadership challenge masquerading as technology problem. Tier 2 (tech company scaling past agility). Revenue above ICP sweet spot but employee count and transformation dynamics make it compelling.

conversation_entries.opening_question: You've committed to maintaining the output of a 10,000-person company with 6,000. In special operations, smaller teams only outperform larger ones with shared consciousness across every unit. How are you building that connective tissue between Square, Cash App, and the ecosystem?

The Naoko Takeda story and employee backlash signal the 'why' behind this transformation hasn't landed. Organizations that succeed invest as much in rebuilding trust as restructuring headcount. What's your plan for the human side?

You're shifting from business-unit autonomy to functional alignment — one of the hardest transitions, especially under time pressure. What mechanisms maintain cross-functional coordination without recreating bureaucracy?

dossiers.recommended_meeting_framing: Frame around 'The Team of Teams Challenge at Block' — not crisis response, but proactive capability build. 'You've made the hardest decision (cutting 40%). The question is whether the remaining org operates like a smaller version of old Block, or something fundamentally better.' Avoid military metaphors until trust is built. Lead with outcomes. Window is NOW — Q1 2026 restructuring charges landing, first 90 days of new reality.

prospect.summary: Once-in-a-century corporate separation — splitting 97-year-old, $24B company with 58K employees across 17 countries into two public companies.
prospect.entry_point: Will Stengel (CEO, former Chief Transformation Officer); Jenn Hulett (CPO, from Dollar Tree)
prospect.conversation_hook: Separation timeline (Q1 2027) means planning happening NOW — optimal engagement window
prospect.company_overview: Genuine Parts Company (GPC) is a global distributor of automotive and industrial replacement parts, operating NAPA Auto Parts in North America and Alliance Automotive Group in Europe. Founded in 1928, GPC serves over 600,000 customers through 10,000+ distribution points worldwide.

dossiers.ownership_structure: Public (NYSE: GPC). 70 consecutive years of increased dividends (Dividend Aristocrat).
dossiers.geographic_footprint: 17 countries, 10,800+ locations. U.S., Canada, Mexico, UK, France, Germany, Belgium, Netherlands, Poland, Australia, New Zealand. Global Automotive (NAPA, Repco, Traction) $15.4B. Global Industrial (Motion) $8.9B.
dossiers.financial_health: Revenue: FY2025 $24.3B (+3.46%). Auto $15.4B, Industrial $8.9B. FY2025 GAAP net loss $(609M) — driven by $825M non-cash pension settlement charge. Adj. net income $216M. FY2026 guidance: $25.0-25.6B (3-5.5% growth), adj. EPS $7.50-8.00. $225-250M transformation costs in 2026. Consensus Buy (33% Strong Buy, 33% Buy, 33% Hold). Raymond James Strong Buy $145; Evercore $160. Pressures: auto margin compression, tariffs, separation execution risk. Tailwinds: $200M savings achieved, separation expected to unlock value, Dividend Aristocrat confidence.
